blocks.text: DTC P2720: Lock-up clutch Short circuit or disconnection of control SOL.V. circuit

The lock-up clutch control SOL.V. controls the hydraulic pressure applied to the lock-up clutch. The spool in the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. pushes a valve in response to the duty (load) output, which is converted to a current value by the PGM-FI ECU. As a result, the valve pushed by the spool releases oil pressure proportional to the current value. the PGM-FI ECU measures the current flowing in the linear solenoid of the lock-up clutch control SOL.V., compares it with the commanded current of the duty output, and feeds back the resulting difference. Failure is caused by disconnection or temporary disconnection. When the drive circuit of the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. is turned on, if the current does not flow to the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a fault and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When the drive circuit of the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. is turned on and no current flows to the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. for more than 1 second.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (LCCLS line) between PGM-FI ECU and lock-up clutch control SOL.V. (including disconnected or loose couplers)
Disconnection of internal circuit of lock-up clutch control SOL.V.

blocks.text: Start the engine and wait at least 1 second at standstill.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
